name: Vilenkin tunneling universe
summary: Proposes that the universe is created by quantum tunneling from a state of zero spatial size into a de Sitter space. The tunneling is described by a Hawking-Moss / de Sitter instanton, after which the universe evolves along the lines of inflationary cosmology. Avoids both a big-bang singularity and an arbitrary specification of initial conditions; the boundary condition is that the tunneling wave function is outgoing only.

addresses_puzzles.note: Combined with inflation, removes the need to specify pre-inflationary initial conditions. Contested because the tunneling boundary condition is mathematically ambiguous (Vilenkin's outgoing-mode prescription vs Hartle-Hawking no-boundary proposal give different probability distributions for inflationary initial conditions).

introduces.note: Boundary-condition prescription on the wave function of the universe.

history: Vilenkin (1982), 'Creation of universes from nothing', Phys. Lett. B 117, 25 — first proposal. Vilenkin (1983), 'Birth of inflationary universes', Phys. Rev. D 27, 2848 — refined formulation. Hartle-Hawking 'no-boundary' wave function (1983) is the main competitor. Quantum-cosmology debate continues without empirical discrimination.

references.citation: Vilenkin, 'Creation of universes from nothing', Phys. Lett. B 117, 25 (1982)
Vilenkin, 'Birth of inflationary universes', Phys. Rev. D 27, 2848 (1983)
